name: DealMirror
tagline: Turn shaky deals into clear next steps.
description: DealMirror helps small businesses and freelancers convert messy sales conversations into actionable, risk-aware deal plans. After a client call, the app ingests notes, emails, or a transcript and identifies hidden deal risks like vague scope, unclear decision makers, delayed timelines, or payment terms that were only implied. It then generates a visual "deal health" snapshot, a follow-up message draft, and a checklist of the exact questions needed to move the deal forward. For daily use, that means a consultant can leave a client meeting and immediately know whether the project is actually ready to close, or a sales rep can see which prospect needs legal review before a contract is sent. A design agency quoting a website rebuild, for example, could discover that the client never confirmed content ownership and get a polite follow-up drafted in seconds. What makes DealMirror unique is that it does not just summarize conversations; it spots business friction points in language, relationships, and commitments, then turns them into concrete next actions that reduce revenue leakage.
features: The app analyzes meeting notes, call transcripts, and email threads to extract commitments, deadlines, objections, and decision-makers, which matters because important deal details often get lost in the rush after a conversation.
It produces a deal risk map that highlights missing scope, budget ambiguity, approval bottlenecks, and payment exposure, so users can prioritize the issues most likely to delay or derail a sale.
It drafts customized follow-up messages that are tactful and specific, helping users confirm next steps without sounding pushy or repetitive.
It generates a closing checklist based on the deal type, such as asking for legal contacts, confirming deliverables, or locking in kickoff dates, which saves time and reduces costly omissions.
It tracks deal momentum over time and alerts users when a prospect is stalling, ensuring they can intervene before a warm lead goes cold.
audience: Independent consultants, small agency owners, freelancers, and early-stage B2B sales teams aged 25-50 who manage high-value client conversations without dedicated sales operations support. It is especially useful for people juggling many prospects at once and needing a faster, smarter way to close work.
useCases: Maya just finished a discovery call with a startup founder and wants to know whether the budget, timeline, and decision maker were actually confirmed before she spends time on a proposal.
Jordan has three active freelance clients and uses the app after each email thread to catch missing approvals, then sends a clean follow-up that keeps the deal moving.

Priya runs a two-person marketing agency and opens the app when a promising lead goes quiet, so she can see whether the blocker is legal review, pricing confusion, or an unanswered question about scope.
